title: Leaving Notes 📝
description: Add comments to explain your code to others (and yourself!).

slides.title: What's a Comment? 💬
slides.content:
# Ignoring Code

Sometimes, you want to write English notes inside your code file to explain what a tricky part does.

But if you just type English, Dart will try to run it and crash!

**Comments** are text that Dart completely ignores when it runs your program.

quizData.question: What happens when Dart encounters a comment?

options.text: It prints the comment to the screen.

quizData.explanation: Comments are meant for humans. The compiler completely ignores them.

slides.title: Single-Line Comments / /
slides.content:
## Two slashes

To make a single-line comment, use two forward slashes `//`.

Anything on that line **after** the `//` will be completely ignored.

`int health = 100; // Starting health for the boss`

quizData.question: Which of these is a correct single-line comment in Dart?
quizData.explanation: Dart uses // for single line comments, just like C, Java, and JavaScript.

slides.title: Multi-Line Comments /*  */
slides.content:
## Big Blocks

If you want to write a huge essay explaining your code, typing `//` on every single line gets annoying.

Instead, use `/*` to start the comment block, and `*/` to end it!

```dart
/* 
This is a really long
comment that spans
across multiple lines! 
*/
```

quizData.question:
Fill in the blank to start a multi-line comment:
__ 
This is a big note!
*/
quizData.explanation: The /* symbol starts a multi-line comment, and */ ends it.

slides.title: See it in Action
